mentions.note: Cramer and Ben covered and felt really good about it; glad they were right; pretty amazing; outstanding positive marks.
Making a move as bear narrative shifts; gave terrific guidance when reported; pessimists starting to tremble; market perception changing.
Travel trust holding; quarter sounded good but market already reached verdict; losing narrative to Walmart and Google on key services (AWS, Prime); doesn't get respect deserved.
Worst performer down 70% after Google Project Genie announcement threatens mobile game ad business; cannot compete with Google; do not own stock.
Like Marvell, sold unfairly as shortage mentality favors memory plays over chip designers; should command higher valuation but market misses the dynamic.
Special-chip supplier caught in irrational market rotation against MRVL and NVDA; great company but unfairly sold due to shortage narrative.
Caller up 725% since 2018; recent body camera catalyst quickly reversed; stock still 'too heavy'—trim further and let rest run without excess weighting.
Caller Rich up 725% since 2018; body camera story provided initial boost but gains immediately erased; stock still too heavy; trim position despite fundamentals.
Agricultural middleman up 28% in January on positive analyst commentary; no specific catalyst identified; trading 13x earnings; wait for Wednesday earnings before deciding.
Rallied from $657 to $690 on ISM strength; industrials group benefits directly from strong economic data.
Down 20% on Trump power cap policy; worth buying on weakness at 24x earnings because new plants take years to build and price gouging is not their strategy.
Down 20% but worth buying weakness; Trump's power-price caps irrelevant given multi-year plant build times; also reaffirmed in Lightning Round.
Crypto volume beneficiary but relationship to price direction ambiguous; risky if margin clients face liquidations.
Up 3% on ISM-triggered retail buying; benefiting from economic strength signals.
Down 20% with multiple compression (30x→16x); Cramer owns in trust, held for years but entire software corridor is 'incredibly bad market' now despite quality fundamentals.
Recommended for enterprise software believers; travel trust holding; but sector in terrible shape with January losses warning against conviction buying.
Caller Michelle raves about DDS as one of best retailers; Cramer doesn't know it well enough; sticking with retail names he knows best.
Up 10% following ISM manufacturing report strongest since 2022; transport sector rallies directly on economic activity strength.
Website tools provider down 19% in January amid software multiple compression (30x→14x earnings); no specific company catalyst beyond valuation concerns.
More magnificent than Tesla per Cramer's implicit comparison; better opportunity than TSLA in auto/EV space.
Only other Mag 7 beater at +68% YTD; fantastic company synonymous with beat-and-raise; Waymo raised $16B at $126B valuation—amazing; reports Wednesday, can blow away numbers.
Medicare Advantage manager hit hardest by CMS rate freeze (flat vs 5% expected); subsidy lag behind inflation creates structural headwind; cannot touch despite smart management.
Third-worst S&P performer in January; Medicare Advantage subsidy freeze by CMS; pricing problems and margin compression; untouchable—can't touch them.
Up 26% recovery despite January beating; Cramer big believer in CEO Lip Boutan; quarter not as negative as perceived; negativity overdone.
Very good company down 25% in January due to multiple compression (30x→20x earnings); overshoot suggests value if you believe in enterprise software industry long-term.
Good company under pressure for no particular reason; acceptable hold but no conviction buy; uranium sector consolidation theme.
Defense play up 31% in January; excellent quarter with top/bottom line beat and terrific forecast; 50% defense budget increase tailwind; CEO Jim Tahet awesome; more upside likely.
Semiconductor capital equipment maker with strong quarter; Cramer big believer in group; suggests legging in gradually despite concerns about oversupply in late 2027-early 2028.
Down 19% on disappointing quarter with weak Macau margins; wealthy Chinese hesitant to spend; Cramer skeptical of gambling sector—prefer US-focused alternative like Wynn.
Down 19% on disappointing quarter with weak Macau margins and elevated promotional spending; wealthy Chinese consumers still hesitant; prefer US-focused casino plays.
Terrific quarter but market narrative stuck on advertising company with Ray-Ban glasses; company not defending or promoting stock; frustratingly indifferent.
Up 50% on 2026 revenue growth return and cancer vaccine potential; but remains deeply unprofitable with years of losses ahead; other biotech like Regeneron preferred.
Excellent company with excellent CEO Matt Murphy making special chips; unfairly punished by 'stupid' shortage-mentality investing (buying SanDisk, selling Marvell); deserves higher valuation.
Excellent company with fabulous CEO Matt Murphy; down from $100 to $70-80; unfairly sold in chip shortage-vs-non-shortage trade rotation; market dynamics are stupid but immovable.
Least magnificent of Mag 7; convoluted conference call; lost AI race to GOOGL; OpenAI relationship undefined/unclear; weak Copilot uptake at 15M users; felt sorry for MSFT.
Bought substantial Bitcoin with borrowed money; reporting this week with unclear leverage exposure risk if crypto declines.
Memory storage play up 45% in January; hottest bull market segment on AI-driven data center demand; tripled in 2025 with strong momentum.
Beat on every key line with strong guidance but fell 10%; multiple compression severe (64x→28x earnings); demonstrates market doesn't value numbers when industry credibility questioned.
Recommended alongside CRM for enterprise software believers, but massive January losses and sector weakness argue against aggressive positioning.
Coiled spring trading at just 24x earnings; up 55% YTD; fundamentals intact; still magnificent despite Mag 7 being dead; buy on weakness maybe tomorrow.
Clobbered on false OpenAI tensions stories; Cramer dismisses criticisms as unfounded; CEO Jensen Wong appearing tomorrow to clarify; stock worth defending.
Raising $45-50B for data center buildout with bond/equity split oversubscribed; positive demand signal but no explicit buy recommendation given.
Cramer looked at over weekend and found nice comeback; prefers to other biotech names; good interview coming with company out in California.
Oil services titan up 26%; drill policy bad for producers but great for services; potential Venezuela exposure; very positive recent outlook when imported.
Beat earnings by 71% ($6.20 vs $3.62 expected) with guidance $12-14/share vs $5-11 consensus; up 143% in January alone; no one knows where top is given magnitude of upside surprise.
Memory storage play up 48% in January; part of hottest data center boom segment driven by AI; more than tripled in 2025.
Caller holds down 96% in IRA; Cramer sees no reason to own; plenty of better companies in telehealth space; don't own.
Up 4% on strong ISM triggering institutional retail buying; new leadership underway.
Robot company rhetoric overblown; XAI/SpaceX merger hype not compelling; hasn't made its move; less magnificent than General Motors; skeptical positioning.
Fired new CFO after just 5 months; CFO uncertainty combined with AI displacement concerns make risk/reward unfavorable.

Public sector software provider down 19% as part of enterprise software multiple compression (54x→29x); no specific company catalyst mentioned.

Storage company benefiting from chip shortage narrative and non-shortage positioning in market rotation; positive side of current trade.
Up 4% on ISM report signaling retail demand strength; new CEO undergoing transition but benefiting from broader retail rally.
